📋 Where Each Tab Gets Its Data

          
Every tab in this dashboard is powered by one of two data stores: KPI Data (metrics from your Excel workbook) or Fuel Data (gallon/aggregator/fleet data per truck stop). Here's the exact mapping:


          

        


        

          
          

            
📊 KPI Data Store

            
Powers: Executive Summary, Financials, Membership, Business Dev, PTP, Rewards, eCred, Vendors

            
How to populate

            

              
Option 1: Excel Import — Export your KPI Dashboard workbook (.xlsx). The "1) Data" sheet should have metric names in Column C with date columns as headers. Go to File / API Import and upload.

              
Option 2: CSV Import — CSV with metric names in the first column and YYYY-MM date headers. Upload via File / API Import.

              
Option 3: JSON Import — Use the JSON schema from Export. Upload or send via API.

              
Option 4: Manual Entry — Go to KPI Data Entry tab in the sidebar and fill in values month-by-month.

              
Option 5: API Auto-Feed — Wire a REST endpoint that returns JSON in the KPI schema. Configure in File / API Import.

            

          


          
          

            
⛽ Fuel Data Store

            
Powers: Network Summary, Aggregators, Fleets, Fuelman, R-Check, Truck Stop Detail, Territory Map

            
How to populate

            

              
Option 1: Manual Entry — Go to Manual Entry tab → Gallon Data Entry. Select a stop, period, enter gallons and aggregator/fleet breakdowns.

              
Option 2: Bulk Entry — Use the Bulk Gallon Entry table on Manual Entry to quickly enter totals for multiple stops.

              
Option 3: JSON Import — Upload a JSON with the fuel schema (stop_id, period, gallons, aggregators, fleets, rack). Upload via File / API Import or topbar.

              
Option 4: API Auto-Feed — Wire a REST endpoint returning fuel JSON. Configure in File / API Import. Set to auto-fetch on page load.

            

          

        


        
        

          
🏢 Vendor Program Data

          
Powers: Vendor Programs tab — participating vendor counts by month

          

            Excel Import — Include a sheet named "Vendor Programs" in your workbook with vendor names in Column A and month columns (Jan, Feb, Mar...). Upload via File / API Import. Also included in JSON import/export.

📥 File Import

            
Import data from Excel (.xlsx), CSV, or JSON. Supports KPI Dashboard workbook format, fuel data, and vendor programs. Data merges with existing records.

            

              
              
            

            

              Accepted formats:

              • KPI Excel — "1) Data" sheet with metric names in Col C, dates in header row

              • Vendor Programs — Sheet named "Vendor Programs" with vendor names in Col A

              • Fuel JSON — Exported from this dashboard or API

              • CSV — Metric names in first column, date columns as headers
            

            
Last Import—

            
KPI Metrics Loaded—

          

          

            
📡 API Connection

            
Wire to a live data source. Auto-refresh on load or on schedule.

            

              
              
            

            

              
              
            

            

              
              
            

            

              
              
            

            

          

          

            
📋 JSON Data Schema

            
Your API endpoint should return data in this format (single record or stops object):

            
{
  "stops": {
    "R01437": {
      "2025-11": {
        "gallons": 2206.76,
        "aggregators": [{"name":"RTS Carrier","id":"3630","processor":"TCH","cost_plus":0.08,"retail_minus":-0.08,"gallons":1376}],
        "fleets": [{"name":"LANDSTAR SAVINGS PLUS","id":"8921","processor":"Comdata","gallons":203.26}],
        "rack": {"rack_index":"Ashland, KY","freight":0.0675,"federal_tax":0.2502,"state_excise":0.234,"misc_tax":0}
      }
    }
  }
}

🌐 Hosting, Sharing & Multi-User Access

          
This dashboard is a single HTML file — it runs entirely in the browser with no server required. But to allow multiple people to access, update, and sync data, you have several options depending on your needs and budget.

        


        

          

            
⚡

            
Option 1: Shared File (Simplest)

            
Host the HTML file on a shared drive (OneDrive, SharePoint, Dropbox, Google Drive). Each user opens it in their browser. Data stays local to each user — no sync.

            
⚠ Best for: Single user or read-only sharing

          


          

            
🔄

            
Option 2: Google Sheets / Airtable Backend

            
Connect this dashboard to a Google Sheet or Airtable as a shared database. Use Apps Script (free) or Airtable API to read/write gallon data. Everyone sees the same data.

            
✓ Best for: Small team, low cost (free tier available)

            
Plugins: Google Apps Script, Airtable REST API

          


          

            
🚀

            
Option 3: Supabase / Firebase (Recommended)

            
Host on Netlify or Vercel (free). Use Supabase (free PostgreSQL) or Firebase as the database. Real-time sync, user auth, API access. No backend code needed.

            
✓ Best for: Team access, real-time updates, API auto-feed

            
Stack: Netlify + Supabase + this file = ~$0/mo

          


          

            
🏢

            
Option 4: Internal Server / WordPress

            
Embed this dashboard in a WordPress page using a custom HTML block. Use the WP REST API + custom plugin to store gallon data in the WP database. Managers log in with their WP credentials.

            
✓ Best for: Existing WP site, IT-managed, role-based access

            
Plugins: Advanced Custom Fields, WP REST API, JWT Auth

          

        


        

          
🔌 How the API Auto-Feed Works

          

            

              
Once hosted, this dashboard can auto-pull gallon data from any REST API that returns the JSON schema shown in the File/API tab. Here's how to set it up:

              
    
                
  1. Host this file at a URL (e.g. roadys.com/dashboard)
    2. 
                
  2. Create a backend endpoint that queries your gallon database and returns JSON
    3. 
                
  3. Paste the URL + API key into the API Connector above
    4. 
                
  4. Set auto-fetch to "Every Page Load"
    5. 
                
  5. Share the hosted URL with your team
    6. 
              

            

            

              
Recommended Free Stack:

              

                
📄
Frontend: Netlify
Drop this HTML file, get a URL instantly. Free. Custom domain supported.

                
🗄️
Database: Supabase
Free PostgreSQL. REST API built-in. Row-level security for multi-user.

                
🔐
Auth: Supabase Auth
Email/password login. Role-based access (manager vs admin).

                
🔄
Sync: Realtime
Supabase Realtime lets multiple users see updates as they happen.

              

            

          

        


        

          
👥 Multi-User Manager Access

          
Each territory manager can have their own login that filters the dashboard to their stops only. Here's the access model:

          

            
Admin
Full access. Assign managers, view all stops, export all data, manage API keys.

            
MSS (Member Support Strategist)
Sees only their assigned states. Can add gallon data, notes, and value props for their stops.

            
Stop Owner
Can view and update their single location. Submits gallon data via web form.

            
Read Only
View-only access to network summary and territory reports. No edits.

          

        

      


      
      

        

          
🏪 Add / Remove Truck Stops

          
Add new locations or offboard existing ones. Changes reflect across all pages including territory maps, MSS assignments, Fuelman/R-Check enrollment, and all metrics.

🗑️ Offboard / Remove Truck Stop

              
Search for a stop and mark as offboarded. This sets status to "offboarded" (data preserved) or fully removes the stop from the system.
